title: Vull A Man
content:
No, Iï¿½m a man, Iï¿½m vull a man,
You beat my manhood, if you can.
Youï¿½ll be a man if you can teake
All steates that household life do meake.
The love-tossï¿½d child, a-croodlen loud,
The bwoy a-screamen wild in play,
The tall grown youth a-steppen proud,
The father staid, the houseï¿½s stay.
No ; I can boast if others can,
Iï¿½m vull a man.

A young-cheakï¿½d motherï¿½s tears mid vall,
When woone a-lost, not half man-tall,
Vrom little hand, a-called vrom play,
Do leave noo tool, but drop a tay,
Anï¿½ die avore heï¿½s father-free
To sheape his life by his own plan;
Anï¿½ vull an angel he shall be,
But here on eï¿½th not vull a man,
No; I could boast if others can,
Iï¿½m vull a man.

I woonce, a child, wer father-fed,
Anï¿½ Iï¿½ve a-vound my childern bread;
My earm, a sisterï¿½s trusty crook,
Is now a faithvul wifeï¿½s own hook;
Anï¿½ Iï¿½ve agone where voï¿½k did zend,
Anï¿½ gone upon my own free mind,
Anï¿½ ofï¿½en at my own witsï¿½ end.
A-led oï¿½ God while I were blind.
No; I could boast if others can,
Iï¿½m vull a man.

Anï¿½ still, ov all my tweil haï¿½ won,
My loven maid anï¿½ merry son,
Though each in turnï¿½s a jay anï¿½ ceare,
ï¿½Ve a-had, anï¿½ still shall have, their sheare
Anï¿½ then, if God should bless their lives,
Why I mid zend vrom son to son
My life, right on drough men anï¿½ wives,
As long, good now, as time do run.
No, I could boast if others can,
Iï¿½m vull a man.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Barnes represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of William Barnes's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
